channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US citizen allegedly carrying cannabis detained in Moscow, charged with narcotics smuggling: Russian media
item.description: An American was detained in Russia for allegedly bringing cannabis-laced gummies through customs at a Moscow airport, Russian media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: Border Patrol agents in the Rio Grande Valley have seen a sharp drop in apprehensions along the southern border since the new administration started.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza discover personal lives shattered by Hamas
item.description: RAMAT GAN, Israel (AP) — After 484 days of captivity in Gaza, Keith Siegel had many questions. Was his 97-year-old mother still alive? Which of his neighbors was killed in Hamas' Oct.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance into eastern Congo's 2nd major city of Bukavu, residents say
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo entered the region’s second-largest city of Bukavu on Friday, local and civil society leaders said, the latest ground gained since a major escalation of their yearslong fighting with government forces.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German prosecutors investigate possible Islamic extremist motive for Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that injured 39 people appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope has respiratory tract infection, is in fair condition and running mild fever, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened and was receiving drug therapy for a respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelensky: US 'didn't want us in NATO' even before Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Friday the U.S. did not want Ukraine to join NATO even before the Trump administration began changing the country's approach toward the Russia-Ukraine war. "The U.S. never saw us in NATO, they just spoke about it.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Suspect in Munich car attack had 'Islamist motivation,' prosecutor says
item.description: The suspect accused of injuring more than 30 people in a car ramming attack in Munich appears to have been motivated by Islamic extremism, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas to free another American in Saturday hostage release
item.description: Hamas has released the names of the next three hostages who will be released on Saturday Feb. 15, including American-Israeli Sagui Dekel-Chen, the second American to be released since Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance says Trump has ‘broad’ range of options in talks with Russia on Ukraine war
item.description: Vice President Vance in a new interview said President Trump has an “extremely broad” range of options, from economic tools to military tools, that he could use of leverage in talks with Russia to end the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches fresh drone attack against Ukraine shortly after Trump-Putin phone call
item.description: Ukraine's air force indicated that there was an overnight drone attack. The news on Thursday came after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said. The blast occurred at the food court on the 12th floor of the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi department store in Taichung city, the Taichung Fire Bureau said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan's 'peace through strength' doctrine can aid Trump administration with Taiwan policy, group says
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute visited Taiwan amidst the U.S. presidential transition, a new administration in Taipei and ongoing Chinese coercion and aggression in Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO is in disarray after the US announces that its security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In just one speech by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth this week, the most powerful member of NATO has thrown the world’s biggest military alliance into disarray, raising troubling questions about America’s commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi educator known for charity and prisoner work wins $1 million Global Teacher Prize
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A Saudi educator known for his charity work and instructing prisoners won the $1 million Global Teacher Prize on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and MALAK HARB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will free more Israeli hostages on Saturday as originally planned
item.description: Hamas announced on Thursday it will free Israeli hostages on Saturday as originally planned, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How South Korea's Constitutional Court is deciding on the president's future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is putting up a desperate fight for his political life at Seoul’s Constitutional Court after being impeached and arrested for his short-lived imposition of martial law last year.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: US releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik in Marc Fogel exchange, official says
item.description: The United States is releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik as part of the deal to secure Marc Fogel's freedom, a Trump administration official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. How to lower your risk
item.description: Polluted air, laden noxious gasses or tiny, invisible particles that burrow into human bodies, kills 7 million people prematurely every year, the U.N. health agency estimates.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
